message:
test(robot-envelope): pin wire format via six golden JSON fixtures

Adds golden-file coverage for `RobotEnvelope<T>` serialization across
the six payload shapes the robot bridge actually emits in the wild,
so a future refactor of the envelope struct (field rename, field
reorder, nested-payload handling change) cannot silently shift the
wire format without a failing test.

## What a "golden fixture" means here

Each test constructs a `RobotEnvelope` with a frozen timestamp and
a representative payload, serializes via `serde_json::to_value`,
runs the result through a small canonicalizer, then compares to a
committed `.json` file under
`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/`.

The canonicalizer handles the bits that would otherwise produce
spurious diffs on re-run:

- `timestamp` string values (at any depth) are replaced with the
  literal `"[TIMESTAMP]"` marker. The fixtures on disk also carry
  `"[TIMESTAMP]"`, so clock skew and wall-time reads do not produce
  churn.
- Arrays nested under the keys `agents`, `tags`, `pane_ids` are
  sorted by serialized-form lexicographically. These are the shapes
  the bridge emits as semantically-unordered sets. Other arrays
  (where order is meaningful) are left alone.
- `f64` values with a fractional part are rounded to 4 decimal
  places. Floating-point round-trip noise at the last ULP is not
  part of the contract we want to pin.

All six fixtures are ~100–285 bytes — small enough to read inline
in a PR review and to commit without bloat concerns.

## Fixtures

- `scalar_payload.json` — `data: 42`. The baseline shape.
- `degraded_payload.json` — `degraded: true` with a structured
  `{"reason": "fallback", "attempts": 3}` payload. Pins the
  degraded-path framing the robot bridge emits on connector failure.
- `float_payload.json` — pins the 4-decimal rounding.
- `sorted_set_payload.json` — pins the agents/tags/pane_ids
  set-sort canonicalization.
- `nested_timestamp_payload.json` — pins the recursive-timestamp
  scrubbing for nested payloads that themselves contain a
  `timestamp` field.
- `structured_payload.json` — end-to-end pin of a realistic pane-
  event-shaped payload.

## Running & updating

Normal test runs compare fixture-on-disk vs. in-memory canonicalized
JSON. To regenerate after an intentional shape change:

    UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
        --test lib robot_envelope::tests::golden_

The `UPDATE_GOLDENS` branch in `assert_golden_json` writes the
current canonicalized bytes back to the fixture path. Commit the
diff as part of the same change that renamed/restructured the
envelope, so reviewers can see both sides.

## Why this is colocated with robot_envelope.rs

`RobotEnvelope` lives in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_envelope.rs`
and is `#[cfg(test)]`-only test glue for fixture loading (new imports:
`serde_json::{json, Value}`, `std::fs`, `std::path::PathBuf`). No
production code path references the fixtures or the canonicalizer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
